source: vidIQ Instagram/TikTok outlier search (5 credits; Instagram section only)
query: A real strange-science phenomenon looks physically impossible in frame zero, then a concise visual mechanism explains why; examples include spherical fire in microgravity
audience: Global English-speaking; ages 18-40; Gen Z/Millennial curious scrollers

reels.creator: @seeing_into_the_unknown
reels.format: static-camera physical demonstration; complete anomaly visible immediately; raw phenomenon-linked audio; looped

reels.creator: @zorancodes
reels.concept: Geiger-counter comparison of uranium and radium
reels.format: POV physical demonstration; instrument response in frame zero; phenomenon-linked sound; looped

reels.creator: @floridafox_anthillart
reels.format: POV macro physical reaction; contact/reaction immediately visible; raw hissing sound; looped

candidatePattern: Three independent Instagram outliers support the broad format of showing a real physical anomaly immediately, keeping the camera/composition simple, and letting phenomenon-linked sound carry part of the evidence. This does not validate spherical fire specifically.
_negativeFinding: The format search returned no microgravity-flame-specific Instagram outlier. No topic-specific trend claim is made.
_discipline: External evidence only. Keep as candidate; do not add to approved-patterns.json without Curio-published evidence or a reviewed qualifying evidence threshold.
